title: Is Eva the only show of its kind that you watch
display_title: Is Eva the only show of its kind that you watch
wikitext:
[]: Is Eva the only show of its kind that you watch?             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Fri Nov 29, 2013 5:28 am I was recently thinking and something crossed my mind, Evangelion is pretty much the only series of it's it's kind that I watch. I usually only watch comedy shows like The Simpsons and Family Guy or straight foward action shows like DBZ and Fairy Tail. Is anyone else like this?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Nov 29, 2013 5:50 am But how do you define the "kind" of show that Evangelion is? I started rewatching the original show last week, after spending the past year or two getting to know a somewhat wide range of anime, and I was immediately struck by the fact that it was from the start distinctly un -like any other show that I have seen.

[]:             Quote Post by Violet Shadow » Fri Nov 29, 2013 7:21 am I enjoy mature themed anime and games; I don't hate others but I like watching these kind of shows because I'm always searching for shows that can really tell me something.

[]:             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Fri Nov 29, 2013 8:03 am I don't watch psychological shows. Only anime I can think of is Paranoia Agent but I'm sure there is plenty more. I'm mostly a gamer and games can't exactly do that sort of stuff like Eva. So I guess thats a no.

[]:             Quote Post by Alpha » Fri Nov 29, 2013 9:48 am I dont think I've ever come across a show that make me think: "This is similar to evangelion". The lord knows I tried to find something that had a similar impact on my interest, but to no avail. I mean, Im interested in other anime, but Evangelion was special and different. It's hard to think of other animes of its "kind".

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Fri Nov 29, 2013 10:53 am Alpha wrote: The lord knows I tried to find something that had a similar impact on my interest, but to no avail.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Nov 29, 2013 3:13 pm pwhodges wrote: But how do you define the "kind" of show that Evangelion is?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Nov 29, 2013 3:42 pm The only other giant robot anime I've ever watched is Big O, which is superficially kinda like Eva in that they're both weird but Big O is about as deep as a puddle and the mysteries are all just gibberish. The fights are awesome though.

[]:             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Fri Nov 29, 2013 4:55 pm It's my first giant robot anime and it's different from my first series Sailor Moon. But, I love both Eva and Sailor Moon despite being two very different shows. I'd watch other shows like Inuyasha, Wedding Peach, Precure, Cutie Honey, etc. but they're just too long. Eva is unique and I love it.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Sat Nov 30, 2013 11:28 am I watch any genre as long it's good. That said, I have to be in a certain mood for something like Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Sat Nov 30, 2013 1:18 pm It's the only mecha anime i ever watched and probably the only mecha anime I’ll ever watch (I just can't take mecha seriously) Although I read "Pluto". Also Evangelion is the only military anime i ever enjoyed.

[]:             Quote Post by desunaito » Sat Nov 30, 2013 8:56 pm Newbie here Im surpriced no one saw Eureka seven as a lighter version of eva i find code geass to be similar as well interm of the fast paced story telling

[]:             Quote Post by banatalie » Sat Nov 30, 2013 10:56 pm I don't watch any anime at all; I've never been interested in anything that I've seen or heard about, until I heard about Eva. Someone told me about the plot and the themes and I was too interested not to try it!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 01, 2013 2:52 am desunaito wrote: Im surpriced no one saw Eureka seven as a lighter version of eva

[]:             Quote Post by Violet Shadow » Mon Dec 02, 2013 1:42 am desunaito wrote: Im surpriced no one saw Eureka seven as a lighter version of eva

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Dec 02, 2013 2:05 am At this point everyone here has probably heard something along the lines of "Madoka is to magical girls as NGE is to mecha".

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Dec 02, 2013 2:36 am ^Heard it? It's pretty much common knowledge to the anime industry.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 02, 2013 2:47 am riffraff11235 wrote: "Madoka is to magical girls as NGE is to mecha".

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Dec 02, 2013 3:09 am Mr. Tines wrote: We shall just have to see if Madoka gets used for selling shaving products a decade from now.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Dec 02, 2013 3:06 pm riffraff11235 wrote: At this point everyone here has probably heard something along the lines of "Madoka is to magical girls as NGE is to mecha".

I usually only watch comedy shows like The Simpsons and Family Guy or straight foward action shows like DBZ and Fairy Tail. Is anyone else like this?
